category: AML - money-mule accounts used to receive and forward illicit proceeds
risk_signals: A recently opened or dormant account suddenly used for rapid pass-through of funds
Many small credits followed almost immediately by withdrawal or onward transfer
Account holder recruited via a job/'work from home' or easy-money scam, with activity mismatched to profile
analyst_reasoning: Mules are the laundering layer of most digital frauds: proceeds land in a mule account and are moved on within minutes to break the trail. The signature is pass-through velocity - money in, money out, little balance retained - in an account whose owner has no economic reason for the flow.
investigation_steps: Measure in/out velocity and residual balance to spot pass-through behaviour
Map the network of upstream senders and downstream beneficiaries to find the mule ring
Check whether the account holder fits a recruited-mule profile (student, new-to-bank, sudden activity)
decision: Freeze the account, file an STR, and trace the network both upstream (victims) and downstream (aggregation points).
outcome: Mule networks fragment and relay stolen funds quickly; freezing early nodes is the main chance to recover victim money.
lessons_learned: Rapid in-and-out pass-through with negligible retained balance is the core mule signal
Mule detection must map networks, not just single accounts
Job-scam recruitment feeds mule supply, so profile mismatch matters
